module-1-terminal-map-project.label: Navigation Checkpoint: Campus Folder Tour
module-1-terminal-map-project.summary: Use pwd, ls, and cd to inspect a prepared folder tree without changing it.

module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.title: Find the club hub
module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.prompt: The school club has a prepared folder tree. Use `ls`, enter `club-hub`, and use `pwd` to confirm you are inside it. Press **Enter** after each command and wait for its result before typing the next one.
module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.hint: Carry the same campus map forward and inspect each stop before changing directories.

help.concept: This project grades a repeatable navigation process using `pwd`, `ls`, and `cd`.
help.hint_1: Carry the same campus map forward and inspect each stop before changing directories.
help.hint_2: Do not invent replacement folders; prove the requested destination with terminal output. The current task asks: The school club has a prepared folder tree. Use `ls`, enter `club-hub`, and use `pwd` to confirm you are inside it.

module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.title: Inspect the newsletter folder
module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.prompt: From the club hub, move into `newsletter`, list its contents, then move back up one folder. Press **Enter** after each command and wait for its result before typing the next one.
module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.hint: Carry the same campus map forward and inspect each stop before changing directories.

module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.prompt: Use what you learned to end inside `club-hub/events`. Run `pwd` and `ls` there so the next teammate knows you found the right place. Press **Enter** after each command and wait for its result before typing the next one.

project-synopsis.title: Campus folder tour
project-synopsis.bodyMarkdown:
The first project is a navigation checkpoint. A campus club has a prepared folder map, and another volunteer needs you to locate specific areas without changing any files.

Work like a careful guide: begin at the workspace root, inspect the available folders, move into the requested location, and report what you find. Each step carries the same map forward, so do not create substitute folders or rename anything.

The project is successful when your command history shows a repeatable inspection routine—`pwd`, `ls`, and deliberate `cd` commands—not a lucky jump to the final folder.
